summary:
The user has provided an extensive transcript detailing Agent2's thought process around why dry heat destroys microorganisms—specifically emphasizing its ability to cause protein coagulation via thermal damage rather than relying on chemical processes like oxidation or hydrolytic cleavage. This finding directly supports option D because multiple scientific references affirm that microbial death under dry heat results predominantly from irreversible structural changes induced in vital enzymes and cytoplasmic components through elevated temperature alone — making it both physiologically accurate and consistent across established literature.
